title: Installable schema descriptions hardcode this project (package names / Muni / in-pi / .project/) — non-portable
description: The packaged installable vocabulary that /context install copies (packages/pi-context/samples/schemas/*.schema.json) leaks THIS monorepo into description text: 8 lines across 5 of 16 schemas hardcode monorepo package names (pi-workflows, pi-project, pi-behavior-monitors, pi-jit-agents), the 'in-pi' runtime assumption, the '.project/' directory, and 'Muni' as the canonical layer model. An installed or exported model therefore carries this repo's identity into other projects, presuming the installer IS this monorepo — directly undercutting the portability arc (R-0002). conception.json + the other 11 schemas are clean. Separately, layer-plans + work-orders are whole-purpose project-specific (their entire subject is this repo's storage-restructuring / JIT-agent machinery), so for them the question is keep-or-drop from a generic catalog, not just description-stripping.

evidence.reference: properties.location.description: 'File path + line number, e.g. packages/pi-workflows/src/dag.ts:219'
properties.package.description: 'Which monorepo package this issue touches (e.g. pi-workflows, pi-project, pi-behavior-monitors)'
description: '...drives the in-pi bounded code-change loop...' — whole description is this repo's JIT-agent/orchestrator machinery
properties.context_blocks.description: '...per the existing ContextBlockRef shape in packages/pi-jit-agents/src/types.ts'
description: 'Capability gaps in the pi-project-workflows framework — ...'
description: 'restructuring the .project/ directory ... conceptual source (e.g. Muni five-layer)' — whole-purpose project-specific
properties.layer.description: 'Which Muni layer the research informs. L1 identity/domain; L2 specification; L3 work; L4 execution; L5 memory.'

impact: Any /context install (or a future export) carries this repo's package names + Muni + .project/ + in-pi into the installing project's schema descriptions — the model presents itself as project-agnostic but is not. Repeats across every consuming project; the leak is the catalog's, so it propagates by design.
proposed_resolution: Genericize the 8 leaking description substrings in samples/schemas/ per a from->to strip-list (package names -> placeholder/'your agent layer'; 'in-pi' -> dropped; '.project/' -> 'substrate storage'; project layer-model proper noun -> 'layered architecture model'/'architecture layer'); preserve the model's intrinsic vocabulary (relations.json, L1-L5 mapping, substrate); produce a portable disinfect markdown; adversarial re-grep to zero residual named-term leaks. RESOLVED at b1cdccc: the 8 substrings genericized across issues/work-orders/framework-gaps/layer-plans/research; zero residual named-term leaks (grep); read-samples-catalog parses all 16 kinds; check + test green; portable strip-list at analysis/2026-06-05-disinfect-schema-descriptions.md. An adversarial re-audit additionally flagged model-intrinsic vocabulary (relations.json / L1-L5 / substrate / feature-story-task) as 'leakage' — dismissed as scope over-reach (that vocabulary describes what the shipped model IS, not which project authored it). Follow-ons NOT part of this closure: .context/schemas/ installed copies carry the same leaks (re-sync via /context install --update); .context/schemas/layer-plans.schema.json is version-skewed (1.0.0, missing layers[]/migration_phases[]).
